source_jobs.title: Robotics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.



WHAT YOU’LL DO



 - Design, develop, test, and debug features for our autonomous construction equipment

 - Analyze robot performance to quantitatively prioritize bug fixes and performance upgrades

 - Optimize controls systems for cycle time and accuracy

 - Develop and maintain code quality standards and best practices

 - Participate in shaping the product road map and deploy code every day



YOUR QUALIFICATIONS



 - BS or MS in computer science or related field

 - 2+ years of software development experience

 - Proficiency in Python

 - Experience with distributed systems

 - Controls experience is a plus, but not required

 - Mechanical engineering experience is a plus, but not required



COMPANY PERKS



 - Competitive salary and equity

 - 100% employer-sponsored health, dental and vision insurance — for you, your spouse and children

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Paid holidays and flexible PTO

 - TSA PreCheck

 - Gym membership

 - Free catered lunch and plenty of snacks

 - Opportunity to work on an 150,000-pound autonomous robot

 - A pair of steel-toe boots



ABOUT THE COMPANY & ROLE



Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.



The starting annual base salary range for this role is $120,000 to $175,000. Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications, working location, and market demands. The total compensation package for this role may also include: equity compensation, benefits such as medical, dental and vision insurance, 401(k) matching, paid time off, cell phone and internet reimbursement and more.



Built Robotics provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. Built Robotics makes hiring decisions based solely on qualifications, merit, and business needs at the time.

source_jobs.title: Senior Robotics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.

 

WHAT YOU’LL DO

 

 - Lead the design, development, and deployment of autonomous capabilities for heavy construction equipment

 - Architect high-level planners for new construction tasks

 - Mentor and guide multidisciplinary engineering teams through technical design reviews and implementation

 - Collaborate closely with hardware, software, and operations teams to integrate robotic systems into the field

 - Improve reliability, safety, and performance of deployed autonomous systems

 - Own technical road maps and help shape long-term product strategy

 

YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 

 - BS, MS, or PhD in Robotics, Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or related field

 - 5+ years of experience developing robotics or autonomous systems

 - Strong proficiency in Python and C++

 - Experience with robotic frameworks such as ROS/ROS2

 - Experience with controls, motion planning, localization, and/or perception systems

 - Strong understanding of distributed systems and real-time software architectures

 

COMPANY PERKS

 

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - 100% employer-sponsored health, dental and vision insurance — for you, your spouse and children

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Paid holidays and flexible PTO

 - TSA PreCheck

 - Gym membership• Free catered lunch and plenty of snacks

 - A pair of steel-toe boots

source_jobs.title: Robot Deployment Lead
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL DO

You will be a leader in the field, responsible for deploying our autonomous equipment and working closely with our partners to ensure that they can operate our products and seamlessly integrate them into their operations. You will identify opportunities to improve the user experience, deploy more robots, and ensure our robots deliver value to our customers. This will also involve establishing a deep understanding of our customers’ needs, priorities, and processes.

In addition, you will work closely with Built’s engineering team, identifying feature developments and recommending future products. Much of our technology also requires running tests on actual hardware, and you will support the engineering team by leading day-to-day operations, debugging, and troubleshooting on the robots.

Finally, you will be representing the Built brand to ensure we always deliver the safest and best product.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Deploying robots to the field and working with our partners while ensuring seamless, safe, and accurate work

 - Working with the engineering team on identifying and reproducing bugs

 - Maintaining equipment, electronics and sensors

 - Assist in testing robots in our facility

 - Be a liaison between operations and engineering

 - Installing sensors and other components on equipment

 - Developing best practices for operations & preventative maintenance

 - Ensuring that our facilities and tools are consistently kept in tidy, safe, and presentable condition

 - Develop knowledge of solar pile driving and related construction activities

 - Representing the Built Robotics brand in the field and working with our customers


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - BS in a technical field or equivalent work experience

 - 5+ years of experience working with, repairing, and maintaining heavy equipment and machinery

 - Excellent hands-on technical ability, including prototype design and fabrication, soldering, metalworking and woodworking

 - Very strong communication skills, especially with operators and customers

 - Rigorous attention to detail

 - Adaptability and flexibility to work in new situations, pick up new skills, and do what it takes to get the job done

 - Ability to drive trucks, operate heavy equipment, and lift 60 lbs

 - Willingness to travel weekly to the field

 - Valid US driver's license


COMPANY PERKS

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - Field bonus — for every day worked in the field, you’ll earn a field bonus of $100 in addition to your base salary

 - 100% employer-sponsored health, dental and vision insurance — for you, your spouse and children

 - Childcare Flexible Spending Account (FSA) to pay for eligible dependent care expenses

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Paid holidays and flexible PTO

 - Paid rotations — select days off to help you recharge

 - TSA Pre Check

 - Gym membership

 - Opportunity to work on an 100,000-pound autonomous robot

 - Jobsite PPE, including work boots of your choice

ABOUT THE COMPANY & ROLE

Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.

The starting annual base salary range for this role is $75,000 - $110,000. Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications, working location, and market demands. The total compensation package for this role may also include: equity compensation, benefits such as medical, dental and vision insurance, 401(k) matching, paid time off, cell phone and internet reimbursement and more.

Built Robotics provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientatio

source_jobs.title: AI & ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.

 

WHAT YOU’LL DO

 

 - Design and develop edge machine learning systems that to improve the autonomy and safety of our construction robots

 - Build scalable ML infrastructure for model training, validation, deployment, and monitoring

 - Develop and optimize computer vision and sensor fusion models for construction environments

 - Analyze large-scale operational datasets to improve model performance and reliability

 - Improve object recognition, scene understanding, and autonomous decision-making systems

 - Integrate cloud-based commercial and open-source AI tools into our autonomy stack

 

YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 

 - BS, MS, or PhD in computer science, artificial intelligence, machine learning, robotics, or related field

 - 3+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning systems

 - Expertise in Python and ML frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow

 - Experience building distributed ML training systems and data pipelines

 - Robotics or autonomous systems experience is a plus

 - Competitive salary and equity

source_jobs.title: Hardware Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.

 

YOUR MISSION

 

As a hardware engineer, you’ll get to re-imagine and design hardware for our fleet of autonomous heavy construction equipment. You’ll have a broad responsibility for scaling our hardware systems and testing for reliability in the field.

 

Our robots are subject to harsh conditions — hot summer days, cold and rainy winters, high vibrations, shock, and dust. You’ll get to tackle these challenges head on, by sourcing new sensors for these rugged conditions and configuring them on the robots for optimal outcome.

You’ll leverage your experience in mechanical and electrical design to increase reliability and longevity of our equipment by analyzing vibration and shock data, designing mounts with sound dynamic and vibrations engineering principles, and design setups for life cycle testing.

 

You’ll create robust and replicable processes to streamline manufacturing of our AI guidance systems, and perform installations on equipment across the United States and Australia. You’ll develop best practices for managing a fleet of hardware systems and will work closely with our software engineers and field team to debug, troubleshoot and resolve complex hardware issues in the field.

 

YOUR QUALIFICATIONS



 - BS, MS, or PhD in mechanical engineering or related field

 - 2+ years of experience using a CAD software design package (e.g. Solidworks, Onshape, Fusion 360)

 - 2+ years of experience in electrical design

 - Familiarity with Python, Linux and ROS is a plus

 - Adaptability and flexibility to work in new situations, pick up new skills, and do what it takes to get the job done

 - Willingness to travel to job sites 1-2 times per month, according to the needs of the business

 

COMPANY PERKS

 

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - 100% employer-sponsored health, dental and vision insurance — for you, your spouse and children

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Paid holidays and flexible PTO

 - TSA PreCheck

 - Gym membership

 - Free catered lunch and plenty of snacks

 - Opportunity to work on an 80,000-pound autonomous robot

 - A pair of steel-toe boots



ABOUT THE COMPANY & ROLE

Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications, working location, and market demands. The total compensation package for this role may also include: equity compensation, benefits such as medical, dental and vision insurance, 401(k) matching, paid time off, cell phone and internet reimbursement and more.

source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.

 

WHAT YOU’LL DO

 

 - Design and build full-stack applications that allow our users to safely control and monitor our autonomous construction robots

 - Develop internal and customer-facing tools for robot command, control, monitoring, and analytics

 - Build scalable backend services and APIs that support robot deployments

 - Develop intuitive front-end interfaces for real-time robotics visualization and control

 - Improve reliability, scalability, and observability across distributed systems

 - Participate in rapid iteration cycles and deploy production code daily

 

YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 

 - BS or MS in Computer Science or related field

 - 3+ years of experience building production-grade web applications and backend systems

 - Experience with React or similar frontend frameworks

 - Experience vibe coding to rapidly iterate on UIs and application functionality

source_jobs.title: Robot Production Lead
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Fabricate prototype assemblies (primarily steel and aluminum) using standard shop tools

 - Prototype quick-turn assemblies

 - Assemble Exosystems and perform installations on autonomous equipment

 - Test hardware assemblies and components, refine and improve designs

 - Perform equipment and facility maintenance

 - Maintain a clean and organized workshop

 - Operate heavy construction equipment for testing

 - Work in-person at our office in San Francisco and at our testing facility in Mojave

 - Excellent hands-on technical ability, including prototype design and fabrication, soldering, and metalworking

 - Attention to detail and accuracy

 - Proficient in data entry, checklists, and organization
